item.arguments: Use $flywheel:setup in a new repo so Flywheel is fully useful before the first real task.
success_definition: Grounds setup in repo truth such as AGENTS.md, manifests, scripts, or workflow files.
Separates required, recommended, and optional setup.
Does not drift into unrelated implementation.

item.arguments: Use $flywheel:setup for a service repo where local development will touch secrets, MCP tools, and permissions.
success_definition: Covers trusted MCP or permission posture.
Still stays grounded in the repo.

item.arguments: Use $flywheel:setup for a web repo where browser proof should be available in normal development.
success_definition: Covers playwright-cli or equivalent browser-readiness checks.
Makes blocked browser proof explicit when tooling is absent.

item.arguments: Use $flywheel:setup doctor in the Flywheel repo when plugin readiness or local eval surfaces may have drifted.
special_constraints: Surface repo-owned doctor or recovery wrappers when present.
success_definition: Mentions repo-owned doctor or recovery surfaces.
Turns the drift into a concrete recovery path.
